artifacts.claimText: Chulalongkorn University supports students and staff developing understanding and appropriate use of AI tools.

artifacts.claimText: For teaching and assessment, Chulalongkorn guidance says instructors should understand selected AI tools, define course-level scope and directions for use, and adjust evaluation when AI use is allowed.

artifacts.claimText: Chulalongkorn guidance says users who use AI tools in a task should cite and clearly state the scope of use; concealing AI-tool use is treated as an ethical violation that may be penalized under relevant rules.

artifacts.notes: Approved as a narrow candidate claim because the Thai official source directly states support for students and staff understanding and appropriately using AI tools.
Approved as scoped teaching guidance; wording preserves the source's should-language for instructors.
Approved because the official Thai source directly links AI-use disclosure to ethical violation risk when concealed.
Approved because the official Thai source uses prohibitive wording for uploading confidential or personal information into AI systems without consent.

artifacts.title: Chulalongkorn University AI Tools Guidance Staged Review
artifacts.summary: Official Chulalongkorn University Thai and English pages dated July 2023 provide university-level principles and guidelines for AI tools. Staged candidate claims cover appropriate AI-tool use, instructor course-level guidance, AI-use disclosure, and confidentiality/personal-information limits. The Thai source is treated as canonical original-language evidence; the English source is retained as a verified companion source.
limitations: This tracker is not legal advice, not academic integrity advice, and not an official university statement unless a linked source is the university's own official page.
This staged run did not use faculty-only or department-only materials for university-wide claims.
OpenClaw staged candidates are not published canonical claims and require downstream review before promotion.
artifacts.sourceRightsPolicy: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.
